text.title: Email Marketing for Solo Operators Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Email Marketing for Solo Operators application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Lists MUST be built from opt-in signups only; purchased or scraped lists are rejected. Complaint rate above 0.1% triggers sanctions.
rule.rationale: Cold lists destroy sender reputation across all future sends and are never recoverable cheaply.

rule.statement: Welcome email MUST fire within 5 minutes of signup, addressed by first name when captured.
rule.rationale: Open rate on welcome is 50-86% — highest of any send; delay collapses it.

rule.statement: Every send beyond the welcome MUST be segmented by ≥1 axis (engagement, source, or behaviour). Broadcast-to-all is rejected after 100 subscribers.
rule.rationale: Unsegmented blasts depress engagement metrics and accelerate inbox-tab demotion.

rule.statement: Each email MUST carry exactly one primary CTA. Multi-CTA emails are rejected — they halve click-through rate.
rule.rationale: Decision fatigue collapses CTR; one ask outperforms three asks combined.

rule.statement: Bounce rate MUST stay <2% and complaint rate <0.1%. Quarterly list-hygiene sweep removes >180-day inactives.
rule.rationale: ESP reputation collapses fast once thresholds are breached; recovery takes weeks.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
